producer_boundary: The simulator verifies replayable primitive artifacts from external string-geometry, vacuum, spectrum, threshold, and verified-numerics producers. It is not itself a string compactification solver.

targets.pass_rule: Every schema, registry, hash binding, artifact reference, row binding, flat-direction classification, and structural invariant passes with no integrity blocker.
targets.failure_effect: Candidate status is INVALID and every scientific aggregate receipt is false.
targets.claim_boundary: Structural packet integrity only; it does not establish any string-physics gate.

targets.pass_rule: Every referenced artifact is a size-bounded regular file below the bundle root, is not a symlink, and matches its declared SHA-256 digest.
targets.failure_effect: Contract integrity and all candidate promotion receipts are false.
targets.claim_boundary: Byte identity and path safety only; a hash does not prove scientific meaning.

targets.pass_rule: The packet target rows match an allowlisted registry ID, version, and hash frozen before candidate evaluation, and the code-owned source-target-separation verifier passes.
targets.failure_effect: Target matching and every rank-based selection receipt remain false.
targets.claim_boundary: Registry identity and precommitment only; it does not prove that a candidate matches the target.

targets.pass_rule: The exact-rational verifier proves an invertible preconditioner, contraction norm below one, strict Krawczyk image containment, and closure of the selected square rows to the full zero system.
targets.failure_effect: Physical local isolation, moduli locking, and the local string-vacuum witness remain false.
targets.claim_boundary: Exact interval algebra conditional on the producer enclosures; physical enclosure semantics require a code-owned verifier.

targets.pass_rule: A code-owned primitive verifier proves that every supplied residual and Jacobian interval encloses the frozen physical evaluator on the entire source box.
targets.failure_effect: Exact interval algebra remains conditional and cannot promote physical isolation.
targets.claim_boundary: Evaluator enclosure only; existence and contraction require separate receipts.

targets.pass_rule: The selected square subsystem is proved to generate the full physical zero system, or every omitted required row has an independent exact identity or full-solution proof.
targets.failure_effect: A contracted subsystem cannot establish a physical root of the full equations.
targets.claim_boundary: Closure of equations only; it does not prove the supplied intervals are valid or contractive.

targets.pass_rule: Every residual direction is independently classified as stabilized or OPH-invisible, with no visible or unclassified direction and no infinitesimal-only claim promoted to a flat family.
targets.failure_effect: Moduli locking and local witness receipts remain false.
targets.claim_boundary: Direction classification only; target matching and existence remain separate.

targets.pass_rule: Contract integrity, interval contraction, and the code-owned augmented-local-isolation semantic verifier all pass.
targets.failure_effect: No physical existence or local-isolation claim is emitted.
targets.claim_boundary: One certified quotient-chart box only; branch-global and catalogue uniqueness remain separate.

targets.pass_rule: A code-owned verifier replays the complete critical-edge CFT, GSO, modular, level-matching, and BRST consistency checks.
targets.failure_effect: Retracts the candidate critical-string lift only.
targets.claim_boundary: Does not establish a compactification, stable vacuum, or low-energy match.

targets.pass_rule: The exact massless cohomology, indices, equivariant projections, representations, Higgs multiplicity, generations, and exotic exclusions are independently replayed.
targets.failure_effect: Retracts the named BD geometric witness.
targets.claim_boundary: Massless-sector certificate only; it is not the heavy spectrum or stabilized vacuum.

targets.pass_rule: The safety symmetry is realized on the same compactification and its action, charges, and anomaly conditions are independently replayed.
targets.failure_effect: Retracts the operator-safe BD extension.
targets.claim_boundary: An abstract MSSM charge assignment is insufficient without geometric realization.

targets.pass_rule: All required Yukawa and Weinberg terms pass, and every forbidden or suppressed baryon-, lepton-, R-parity-, dimension-five-, and mu-term channel is replayed at the declared perturbative order.
targets.failure_effect: Retracts operator-safe candidate status.
targets.claim_boundary: Operator selection does not establish masses, thresholds, or vacuum stability.

targets.pass_rule: The complete threshold-relevant spectrum and certified tail, normalized couplings, common-scheme matching, RG flow, breaking or nonsupersymmetric continuation, and decoupling map all pass.
targets.failure_effect: No low-energy compatibility or threshold claim is emitted.
targets.claim_boundary: Displayed target-side proxy numbers never satisfy this receipt.

targets.pass_rule: The completed constraint locus, physical quotient, stationary point, route-appropriate scalar stability margin, and effective-field-theory control bounds all pass.
targets.failure_effect: The candidate has no certified physical vacuum slice.
targets.claim_boundary: Stability does not by itself prove observable identifiability or target matching.

targets.pass_rule: The completed stable physical slice passes, the target is source-independent and matched, and exact interval replay proves full constraint-augmented transverse rank and local isolation with no visible or unclassified flat direction.
targets.failure_effect: Retracts selected-candidate status while leaving the structural BD row and recovered OPH core unchanged.
targets.claim_boundary: Local target isolation only; no branch or catalogue uniqueness follows.

targets.pass_rule: Every target-independent worldsheet, compactification, safety, anomaly, vacuum, spectrum, threshold, continuation, and quotient gate passes.
targets.failure_effect: The candidate is not a physically consistent local string vacuum, independent of OPH target matching.
targets.claim_boundary: Candidate consistency only; it does not establish OPH target matching or selection.

targets.pass_rule: Every candidate semantic gate, structural dependency, stability check, interval-isolation check, target row, and declared blocker passes; candidate_status is PASS.
targets.failure_effect: No local OPH-correct string-vacuum witness is emitted.
targets.claim_boundary: One local candidate witness; it is not comparative or globally unique.

targets.pass_rule: A code-owned verifier proves that every candidate in the declared scope is represented.
targets.failure_effect: Comparative uniqueness remains inconclusive.
targets.claim_boundary: Coverage is limited to the declared scope unless an unrestricted reduction theorem also passes.

targets.pass_rule: A code-owned verifier proves a complete, nonoverlapping partition into physical OPH-equivalence classes, including declared dualities.
targets.failure_effect: Presentation duplicates or missed dual descriptions block uniqueness.
targets.claim_boundary: Equivalence classification only; it does not prove any candidate passes.

targets.pass_rule: Every candidate evidence packet is hash-verified, independently re-evaluated, and byte-for-byte consistent with its supplied verification report.
targets.failure_effect: Catalogue status is INCONCLUSIVE.
targets.claim_boundary: Replay consistency only; PASS, FAIL, and INCONCLUSIVE remain scientific outcomes of each candidate verifier.

targets.pass_rule: Every catalogue row has semantically verified PASS branch coverage. FAIL or INCONCLUSIVE coverage blocks selection and cannot suppress a passing candidate.
targets.failure_effect: Comparative uniqueness remains inconclusive.
targets.claim_boundary: Global only on each declared branch, not across an undeclared candidate universe.

targets.pass_rule: Every covered box, boundary, singular stratum, discrete completion, and noncompact tail has a replayed terminal PASS or FAIL verdict; any unresolved cell blocks the receipt.
targets.failure_effect: No branch can be excluded or selected globally.
targets.claim_boundary: Terminal branch-cell verdicts only; catalogue enumeration and equivalence remain separate.

targets.pass_rule: On a passing branch, exactly one target-matching physical equivalence class remains after every other branch cell receives a terminal exclusion verdict.
targets.failure_effect: A local witness cannot be promoted to branch-global uniqueness.
targets.claim_boundary: Uniqueness on one declared branch only.

targets.pass_rule: The declared catalogue and equivalence partition are complete, every candidate and branch result is terminal and replayed, no region is unresolved, and exactly one physical equivalence class passes.
targets.failure_effect: No catalogue-relative string selection is emitted.
targets.claim_boundary: Selection is only within the semantically verified declared catalogue scope.

targets.pass_rule: A code-owned verifier proves that every eligible string presentation is represented in the declared catalogue up to the verified physical equivalence relation.
targets.failure_effect: The result remains catalogue-relative.
targets.claim_boundary: This is the extra proof needed to extend catalogue selection to the declared unrestricted string universe.

targets.pass_rule: The replayed claim DAG has no dependency path from any string-selector receipt into the recovered OPH-core receipts, and every selector failure effect is scoped to the string continuation.
targets.failure_effect: The simulator may report string-selector results but cannot certify their claimed independence from the OPH core.
targets.claim_boundary: Claim-scope independence only; it does not make any string candidate pass.

targets.pass_rule: Exactly one physical OPH-equivalence class passes a complete catalogue and the unrestricted reduction theorem is semantically verified.
targets.failure_effect: No claim to the correct or globally selected string theory is emitted; narrower receipts retain their own scope.
targets.claim_boundary: Selects a physical equivalence class, not a unique perturbative presentation, and does not alter the recovered OPH core if false.
